published_prior_art.cite: WangLiYu2025, HemerikGoeman2018, and DobribanYu2025 provide adjacent causal-conformal or symmetry machinery, not a universal two-arm observable-selector orbit-mass iff.

RamdasBarberCandesTibshirani2023 validates arbitrary randomized permutation references; the convex-hull feasibility equivalence is elementary, but its one-common-target two-arm application is distinct.

TibshiraniBarberCandesRamdas2019 supplies weighted-exchangeability likelihood-ratio conformal prediction; the known-randomization orbit-selector instantiation is distinct but not a new generic weighting principle.
BarberCandesRamdasTibshirani2023 already studies coverage degradation beyond exchangeability; the finite arm-orbit total-variation certificate is a specialized reduction.
The k=M+1 infinite-threshold conclusion follows directly from ordinary split-conformal rank inversion.
Support mismatch is a known weighted-conformal failure mode; the bounded common-target two-arm orbit witness is a useful specialization.
BaiLiuShaikhTabordMeehan2024 supplies the pair-matched assignment representation; this is an algebraic reduction inside the new selector framework.
This is a concrete validation instance of the proposed certificate rather than a separately broad theorem advance.

DobribanYu2025 and BarberCandesRamdasTibshirani2023 do not supply the finite, coupled two-arm common-target orbit frontier; JinRen2024 is relevant unpublished selection-conditional conformal work, not a published subsuming result.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: All finite-coverage statements
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Coverage is conditional on iid replicated matched-block superpopulation arrays, independent external training, prespecified target selection, and auxiliary selector draws; it is not design-based coverage for the single Shamba Maisha finite population.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The unweighted and weighted coverage claims are accepted only in this replicated-array conditional regime.

source_verification_receipts.version: Official Statistica Sinica preprint SS-2025-0476; current page status FUTURE
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The official preprint states finite-sample conformal causal inference for cluster randomized trials, prediction intervals for counterfactual differences at cluster and individual levels, and compatibility with arbitrary working outcome models.
The paper is an exact random-permutation testing result based on group-structured transformations.
The paper develops weighted conformal prediction under known or estimable test-to-training likelihood ratios and weighted exchangeability.
The paper gives counterfactual and ITE intervals with finite-sample average coverage in completely randomized or stratified randomized experiments with perfect compliance.
Theorem 2.2 validates p-values based on iid permutations drawn from an arbitrary distribution.
The paper studies conformal coverage robustness when exchangeability fails, including weighted-quantile and randomization devices.
SymmPI gives predictive inference under general distributional group symmetries, proves coverage under invariance, and characterizes distribution-shift performance.
The paper defines matched-pair cluster randomization as pairing clusters and randomly selecting one cluster in each pair for treatment, and studies inference and pairwise randomization tests.
The study selected 16 Kenyan health facilities in eight matched pairs and randomized facilities within pairs to intervention or control.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add JinRen2024 to related work as unpublished selection-conditional conformal work and state precisely why its permutation-invariant selection framework does not produce the common two-arm orbit certificate.

Add a strict comparison proposition showing when the LeiCandes2021 unit-level weighted construction agrees with the orbit-selector construction and a pair-matched regime where it cannot implement one coherent target selector.

Develop the frontier conjecture into an explicit finite optimization algorithm for pair blocks, including input size, constraints, and a nontrivial worked instance beyond the disjoint-support boundary.

recommended_next_step: Advance this field-tier proposal to Stage 0, retaining the accepted replicated-array scope and prioritizing the finite frontier algorithm.
